meta.stateless_note: Analysis is deterministic and in-memory. No model was used, no data was retained, and no data left this machine.

atomicwork_capabilities: AI coworker in Slack or Microsoft Teams

backlog.note: Physical fulfilment caps automation; focus on request intake, approval, and status.

agentic_backlog.trigger: Password reset, account unlock, or access request ticket created
agentic_backlog.required_permissions: Scoped identity admin for reset/unlock/group membership
agentic_backlog.automation_feasibility: High where IdP API is available
agentic_backlog.risk_level: Medium (identity actions; privileged changes need approval)
agentic_backlog.fallback_path: Route to IAM team with a prepared action packet
agentic_backlog.human_approval_required: Required for privileged or role escalations
agentic_backlog.expected_impact: Large share of L1 access load handled without an agent

agentic_backlog.trigger: Repeated application error or configuration request
agentic_backlog.system_of_action: Application admin console or config API
agentic_backlog.automation_feasibility: Medium; depends on app API maturity
agentic_backlog.fallback_path: Escalate to app owner with diagnosis
agentic_backlog.expected_impact: Deflect how-to and known-error tickets, shorten diagnosis

agentic_backlog.trigger: CRM data/metadata sync issue or record correction
agentic_backlog.expected_impact: Resolve recurring CRM sync/data tickets faster

agentic_backlog.trigger: Device enrolment, compliance, or remote action request
agentic_backlog.expected_impact: Deflect enrolment/compliance tickets; act on devices safely

agentic_backlog.trigger: Distribution list, mailbox, or calendar access request

agentic_backlog.automation_feasibility: High where attendance API/UI is reachable
agentic_backlog.expected_impact: Deflect routine attendance/leave tickets end to end

agentic_backlog.trigger: Recurring report request or data reconciliation
agentic_backlog.required_permissions: Read plus scoped write for reconciliation
agentic_backlog.expected_impact: Deflect standard report requests; speed reconciliation

agentic_backlog.trigger: Payroll or benefits query or correction request
agentic_backlog.required_permissions: Read plus scoped correction with approval
agentic_backlog.expected_impact: Deflect policy questions; speed corrections with a human checkpoint

capabilities: AI coworker in Slack or Microsoft Teams

assumptions: Deflection ranges assume the resolution path is API-accessible and knowledge quality is good.
Ranges are planning estimates, not commitments; validate with SMEs before quoting.
Physical fulfilment (hardware) and ambiguous 'Other' tickets are excluded from deflection upside.

current_state_summary: Largest theme: Access & Authentication at 23.2% of volume.

top_findings.finding: 7 themes drive ~80% of ticket volume (led by Access & Authentication at 23.2%).
Slowest theme by MTTR: Hardware & Devices at 42.2h median.
Highest deflection upside: Access & Authentication (56-97 tickets, Workflow Automation).

actions: Stand up the AI coworker in Slack/Teams
Deflect top how-to and password/access requests

days_60_90.focus: Integration and agentic use cases with guardrails
actions: Pilot one agentic backend use case with human approval

risks_and_assumptions: Deflection ranges assume the resolution path is API-accessible and knowledge quality is good.
Ranges are planning estimates, not commitments; validate with SMEs before quoting.
Physical fulfilment (hardware) and ambiguous 'Other' tickets are excluded from deflection upside.
Data quality is Strong; low-confidence themes need SME validation.
workshop_questions: Which of the top themes are already partially automated today?
For the highest-volume theme, is the resolution path API-accessible or admin-UI only?
What is the knowledge-base quality and coverage for the top deflection candidates?
Which actions require a human approval step for compliance or risk reasons?
For Access & Authentication, who owns the system of action and what permissions can be granted?
final_recommendations: Start with quick wins: Access & Authentication, Application & Software, Email & Collaboration. These are deterministic and low risk.
Prioritize Access & Authentication for the largest deflection upside (56-97 tickets).
Pilot one agentic use case (Access & Authentication) behind a human approval checkpoint before scaling.
Treat all deflection figures as ranges to validate with SMEs, not commitments.

cases.title: Full lifecycle for a Access & Authentication ticket
steps: As a requester, raise a incident representative of the 'Access & Authentication' theme
Verify it is categorized and routed to the right queue
As an agent, assign it, work it, and add a resolution note
Move it through the status flow (Open -> In Progress -> Resolved) and close it
Verify the requester can see the state at each step
cases.expected: Ticket completes the full flow; every transition is recorded and visible to the requester
cases.source: theme 'Access & Authentication': 139 tickets (23.2% of volume)

cases.source: theme 'Application & Software': 108 tickets (18.0% of volume)

cases.title: SLA behavior for priority 4 - Low
steps: Create a test ticket at priority 4 - Low
Verify the SLA clock, target, and any escalation timer start correctly
Let the ticket approach breach in the test window if feasible
Verify breach warnings fire to the right people
cases.expected: Priority 4 - Low applies the configured SLA and escalation path
cases.source: priority '4 - Low' present in the data

cases.title: Escalation path for the historically slowest area (Hardware & Devices)
steps: Create a ticket in the 'Hardware & Devices' area
Leave it unactioned past the escalation threshold
Verify it escalates to the manager or next tier automatically
cases.expected: Stale tickets escalate instead of sitting idle
cases.source: slowest theme by MTTR: Hardware & Devices at 42.2h median

cases.title: Requester sees only their own tickets
steps: Log in as a plain requester test account
Verify they can raise and view their own tickets
Verify they cannot see other users' tickets, admin settings, or queues
cases.expected: Requester access is limited to their own records
cases.source: baseline role separation

cases.title: Queue visibility and assignment for group 'Endpoint Team'
steps: Log in as a test agent in 'Endpoint Team'
Verify the agent sees the 'Endpoint Team' queue and can pick up and reassign within it
Verify the agent cannot edit tickets owned exclusively by other groups unless that is intended
cases.expected: 'Endpoint Team' agents work their queue; cross-queue access matches the agreed design
cases.source: assignment group 'Endpoint Team' present in the data

cases.title: Acknowledgement on ticket creation
steps: Before running: confirm outbound email is disabled or redirected to internal test recipients
Raise a test ticket
Verify the requester receives (or would receive) a creation acknowledgement
Verify the notification goes only to the intended recipient
cases.expected: Creation acknowledgement is sent to the requester only
cases.source: standard notification flow; email-safety guard applied

cases.title: Assignment and resolution notifications
steps: Before running: confirm outbound email is disabled or redirected to internal test recipients
Assign a test ticket to an agent and verify the agent is notified
Resolve it and verify the requester is notified with the resolution
cases.expected: Assignment notifies the agent; resolution notifies the requester
cases.source: standard notification flow; email-safety guard applied

cases.title: No notifications leak outside the test group
steps: Review the notification/audit log after a full test day
Verify no notification was sent to a real end user or external address
cases.expected: Zero notifications reached anyone outside the test group
cases.source: risk raised for go-lives without a test environment

cases.title: Approval flow for a Approval / Workflow request
steps: Raise a request in the 'Approval / Workflow' area that requires approval
Verify the approver is notified and can approve or reject
Verify rejection returns the request with a reason
Verify approval releases the request for fulfilment
cases.expected: Approve and reject paths both work and are recorded
cases.source: theme 'Approval / Workflow' present in the data

cases.title: Approval cannot be bypassed
steps: As an agent, attempt to fulfil an approval-gated request before approval
Verify the tool blocks fulfilment until the approval is recorded
cases.expected: Approval gate cannot be skipped
cases.source: control test for the approval flow

cases.title: Operational report matches known baseline
steps: Run the volume-by-category and open-backlog reports on the test data
Compare against the counts from this analysis as the baseline
cases.expected: Report figures reconcile with the baseline counts
cases.source: baseline: 600 records analyzed

cases.title: MTTR/resolution-time report is computable
steps: Resolve at least two test tickets with known timestamps
Run the resolution-time report and verify the durations are correct
cases.expected: Resolution time is computed from the correct timestamps
cases.source: MTTR available in the historical data

cases.title: Migrated record counts reconcile
steps: Export the source data (baseline: 600 records)
Import into the new tool
Verify counts match by status and by category; investigate any delta
cases.expected: Source and target record counts reconcile exactly
cases.source: migration from the current email/excel workflow

cases.title: Missing fields are captured going forward
steps: Verify the new tool makes these fields mandatory or defaulted at creation: assignee, mttr_hours
Raise a test ticket and confirm the fields are populated
cases.expected: Fields absent in the historical data are captured by the new tool
cases.source: fields missing in the source data: assignee, mttr_hours

test_plan.coverage_note: Cases are generated from the 11 themes, 6 assignment groups, and 4 priority levels observed in the data. Test how the team actually works, not every feature.

can: Configure workflows, SLAs, notifications, users, roles

raci.activity: Map current workflows into test scenarios
Configure the tool (queues, roles, SLAs, notifications)
Prepare test accounts and disable/redirect outbound email
Execute UAT test cases and log defects
Data migration dry run and reconciliation

readiness_checklist.item: Outbound email/notifications disabled or redirected to internal test recipients for the whole test window
readiness_checklist.source: no test environment: prevent accidental customer emails

readiness_checklist.item: Dedicated test accounts exist for every role in the role matrix
All Must test cases passed and signed off by a named owner
Rollback plan agreed (keep the old email/excel channel warm for the first two weeks)
Support model for launch week defined (who triages, who escalates)
End users trained on raising and tracking requests
Category scheme in the new tool covers the top observed themes: Access & Authentication, Application & Software, Salesforce / CRM, Hardware & Devices, Device Management / MDM
readiness_checklist.source: theme breakdown of the historical data

readiness_checklist.item: Request types configured to match observed intake: Incident, Request
readiness_checklist.source: type field in the historical data

readiness_checklist.item: Baseline metrics from this analysis saved to compare after go-live (volume, MTTR, theme mix)

activities: Confirm configuration: queues, categories, roles, SLAs
Create test accounts per role; disable or redirect outbound email
Smoke test: one ticket end to end per request type
phase_plan_15_day.exit_criteria: One ticket completes the full lifecycle with no blocker

activities: Run lifecycle cases for the top themes (Access & Authentication, Application & Software, Salesforce / CRM, Hardware & Devices)
Log every defect with steps to reproduce; retest fixes daily
phase_plan_15_day.exit_criteria: All Must lifecycle and SLA cases pass

activities: Verify queue visibility for each group (Endpoint Team, HR Ops, CRM Support, Network Team, Service Desk L1, ERP Support)
Run all notification cases; audit that nothing leaked externally
Run approval flow and bypass-prevention cases
phase_plan_15_day.exit_criteria: Role matrix verified; zero external notification leaks

activities: Dry-run data migration; reconcile counts against the baseline
Regression pass on all previously failed cases
phase_plan_15_day.exit_criteria: Migration reconciles; no open Must defects

activities: Walk the readiness checklist with the delivery lead
Named sign-off on every Must item
Decide go/no-go; if no-go, agree the gap plan
Re-enable outbound notifications for production recipients
Final comms to end users; hypercare rota confirmed
Keep the old intake channel open as fallback
phase_plan_15_day.exit_criteria: Tool live; fallback and support rota in place

assumptions: Test in a sandbox if available. If not, disable outbound email/notifications or redirect them to internal test recipients before any test run, so no customer or end user is notified by accident.
Use dedicated test accounts per role, never personal or customer accounts.
Test cases reflect workflows observed in the historical data; new processes introduced by the tool need cases added by the process owner.
Every Must case needs a pass and a named sign-off before go-live.
